summary: The implementation provides a comprehensive CSE Roadmap Tracker with Internet Identity authentication, React/TypeScript frontend with Tailwind CSS, and Motoko backend. Most requirements are implemented including protected routes, dashboard with analytics, task management, project tracking, and mock test tracking. However, several key features are missing: Timeline view (REQ-4), Progress Tracking page (REQ-8), User Profile endpoints (REQ-9), and dummy data initialization (REQ-17). The diff is truncated so some implementations may exist but are not visible.

missing_requirements.requirement: Build Timeline view with collapsible accordion structure organized by Month → Week → Day, allowing users to track daily progress and mark topics as complete
missing_requirements.reason: Timeline.tsx page file is referenced in routing but the actual page implementation is not included in the diff. Backend has TimelineDay type defined but no endpoints for CRUD operations on timeline entries.

missing_requirements.requirement: Create Progress Tracking page displaying overall completion percentage, comprehensive graphs, and weekly performance trend analytics
missing_requirements.reason: ProgressTracking.tsx page is referenced in routing but the actual implementation is not visible in the diff. No specific analytics aggregation beyond basic task counts is evident in the backend.

missing_requirements.requirement: Implement backend User Profile module with get profile and update profile endpoints
missing_requirements.reason: Backend shows getProfile() endpoint but it is incomplete (only returns profile, no update endpoint visible). The profile retrieval traps if profile not found with no creation mechanism shown.

missing_requirements.requirement: Include example dummy data for tasks, projects, mock tests, and timeline entries to demonstrate full functionality
missing_requirements.reason: No seed data initialization logic is visible in the backend. The backend initializes empty data structures but includes no dummy/example data for demonstration purposes.

hallucinated_features.description: Theme provider with dark mode support
hallucinated_features.reason: ThemeProvider from next-themes is implemented in App.tsx with system theme support, but this was not mentioned in requirements. Requirements specified Tailwind CSS but not theme switching functionality.
ProjectStatusChart.tsx implements a pie chart for project status distribution, but REQ-6 only specified displaying projects with status updates, not visualizing status distribution in charts.
